exemplar.title: To what extent do visual and literary rhetorics within smoking advertisements target youth audiences across generations?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do visual and literary rhetorics within smoking advertisements target youth audiences across generations?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Research question is explicitly stated, narrow in scope, and consistently sign-posted throughout the investigation
Clear communication of purpose and focus, with section headings directly tied to the RQ
Generally coherent methodological description with consistent citation of primary visual artefacts and secondary literature
weaknesses: No justification for the sampling strategy used to select the two campaigns and individual images
Temporal scope (âacross generationsâ) is undefined, reducing methodological precision
Research question spans multiple paragraphs in the introduction, disrupting coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (Topic communication; Research question focus) clearly meet the 5â6 band with accurate, effective communication and focus, but the methodological strand sits in the 3â4 band due to an incomplete source-selection rationale and limited methodological range. Averaging these performances and weighting the higher clarity/focus merits a mid-band score of 5.

strengths: Good, coherent knowledge of advertising history and effective integration of credible peer-reviewed sources (e.g., JAMA study)
Accurate and mostly consistent use of subject-specific terminology (ethos/pathos, rule of thirds, negative space)
Broad range of primary and secondary sources evident in the Works Cited
MLA referencing conventions are generally met
weaknesses: Theoretical framing is limited â no sustained engagement with branding or advertising theory to deepen analysis
Occasional conflation of terms (e.g., âmise-en-sceneâ vs. âvisual narrativeâ) introduces conceptual ambiguity
Application of some sources is illustrative rather than critically interrogated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates good knowledge and mostly accurate use of terminology, placing it solidly in the 3â4 band. Gaps in deeper theoretical application and occasional conceptual imprecision prevent a top-band award, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Comparative analysis synthesizes similarities and differences across campaigns with clarity and insight
Nuanced links between visual/literary features (e.g., slogans, colour schemes) and intended demographic effects
Contextual awareness of inclusivity, social norms, and evolving regulatory frameworks
Strong evidence of critical synthesis, especially in sections on cultural connotations and campaign evolution
weaknesses: Limited critical evaluation of source limitations and methodology â few reflections on validity or bias
Discussion occasionally lapses into speculative language without corroboration
Evaluation of counter-evidence and methodological shortcomings remains cursory
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall within the 4â6 band: research is relevant but not exhaustive (5), analysis is mostly supported (5), and evaluation is partially critical (4). Given the number and depth of strong evaluative insights, a high-end band score of 6 is merited, rewarding the excellent comparative synthesis despite some evaluative gaps.

strengths: All required structural elements (title page, contents, sectional headings, conclusion, works cited) are present and logically ordered
Figures are placed close to discussion and consistently captioned
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers, reducing navigability
Occasional formatting inconsistencies (broken URLs, low-resolution images)
Long uninterrupted paragraphs impede readability and would benefit from sub-headings or shorter blocks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure meets the 3â4 band expectations, but layout and formatting errors (TOC omissions, URL breaks, inconsistent figure quality) place the work in the 1â2 band. An overall Presentation score of 3 balances strong structural compliance with notable formatting weaknesses.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student explains using primary visual artefacts plus secondary academic sources (JAMA study, Tobacco Control articles) and cites them consistently.  However, only two campaigns and a modest pool of secondary literature are sampled; there is no explicit justification of source selection strategy, no discussion of alternative methodological tools (e.g., coding frameworks, interviews) and limited explanation of how images were chosen from the 200-item corpus.  The range is therefore âmostly completeâ rather than comprehensive, meriting the upper half of the 3-4 band.

The layout is mostly consistentâ12-pt font, double spacing, headings differentiated, figures centred.  Pages appear numbered in extraction, and MLA in-text citations are largely accurate.  However, some URLs break mid-line, a few figure files are low-resolution, and there is occasional inconsistent indentation in the works-cited list, so not all formatting conventions are flawlessly applied.  This places the essay in the upper tier of the 1-2 band, warranting the maximum 3 within that range.

Primary and secondary research is adequate and largely relevant: a visual corpus, corporate webpages, peer-reviewed articles, and statistical studies all tie back to the question of youth targeting.  Some references (e.g., company websites) are uncritical, and additional scholarly perspectives on rhetoric or youth psychology would strengthen scope, so âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ research quality is evidencedâhence a mid-band score of 5.

The student immediately identifies a clear area of investigationâhow visual and literary rhetoric in smoking advertisements targets youthâand restates this focus coherently throughout the introduction, headings, and conclusion.  Purpose and scope (two well-defined campaigns, precise word limit, English A Cat 3) are sign-posted and maintained, so the reader is never in doubt about what is being explored.  Minor diffuseness remains around the phrase âacross generations,â which is not fully operationalised, preventing a top-band award, but overall clarity, relevance and sustained focus justify a strong 5 within the 5-6 band.

Arguments are generally sustainedâintroduction sets hypotheses, body sections analyse, comparative segment synthesises, conclusion revisitsâbut evaluation is intermittent.  The student acknowledges limitations (âa definitive conclusion cannot be reachedâ) yet offers only cursory reflection on methodology or counter-evidence.  Critical evaluation is therefore âpartiallyâ present, aligning with the lower end of the 4-6 band (score 4).

The student moves beyond description by linking visual features (colour contrast, camera angle) to intended demographic effects and comparing temporal shifts in regulation.  Most claims are anchored in examples and citations, showing logical progression.  Nonetheless, some sections lapse into narrative or speculative language (âthis can ignite a feeling of reliefâ) without corroboration, so conclusion support is âmostlyâ rather than fully secure.  Band 4-6, score 5, is appropriate.

All mandatory EE structural elementsâtitle page, abstract substitute (word-count line), contents, sectional headings, conclusion, works citedâare present and logically ordered.  Figures are placed near commentary and captioned.  Minor issues (table of contents lacks page numbers; captions occasionally omit full MLA details) are insufficient to drop the work below the top band; hence 4 points.

The essay shows good knowledge of advertising history (e.g., Camelâs 75th anniversary, BATâs 2035 smokeless target) and deploys credible studies to support claims.  Source material is correctly cited and usually integrated, though sometimes used illustratively rather than analytically (e.g., health-impact statistics are quoted but not interrogated).  Gaps in theoretical framing (no sustained engagement with advertising theory beyond basic rhetoric) keep performance within, not above, the 3-4 band; the stronger end (4) is justified by generally coherent understanding.

Subject terminologyâethos/pathos, copy, slogan, rule of thirds, negative spaceâis employed frequently and usually accurately.  Concepts such as âmasculinity,â âinclusive image,â and âconsumer confidenceâ are applied with reasonable precision, and MLA referencing conventions are mostly met.  Occasional vagueness (e.g., conflating âvisual narrativeâ with âmise-en-sceneâ) prevents the consistency expected for the top band, situating achievement solidly at 4.

The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and repeated verbatim in the introduction.  It is appropriately narrow for 4 000 words (two specific advertising campaigns) and underpins section titles such as âComparative Analysis,â demonstrating connection to discussion.  Some conceptual imprecisionâe.g., the undefined temporal scope of âacross generationsââslightly limits precision, so a mid-band 5 is most fitting.

annotations.comment: The methodology for selecting the two campaigns and image samples is not justified. Add rationale for source selection and sampling strategy.
The title is clear but overly generic. Consider refining it to directly reference the specific campaigns and rhetorical focus to increase precision.
Consider adding page numbers to the table of contents to adhere to structure conventions and improve reader orientation.
The student successfully restates the research question in the introduction, strengthening focus alignment.
The health-impact statistic is cited accurately but not critiqued. Engage with study limitations or context to deepen analysis.
The research question is explicitly stated and appropriately focused on two campaigns, demonstrating strong connection to the investigation scope.
The research question spans two separate paragraphs, which disrupts clarity. Combine into a single block to maintain coherence.
The student provides a precise word count (3 922), meeting formatting requirements at the document start.
The table of contents lists all sections but lacks page numbers, reducing navigability for the reader.
The introductionâs opening sentence engages effectively with the topic, setting a clear scene for the investigation.
Figure 3âs caption lacks full MLA details for the website; ensure each caption mirrors Works Cited entries.
The allusion to U.S. founding fathers is nuanced, showing depth in critical evaluation of visual symbolism.
Descriptions of the Joe Camel campaignâs success are informative but largely descriptive. Link those metrics to specific rhetorical techniques for more depth.
Figure 1âs caption follows MLA but omits consistent access-date formatting; ensure uniform citation style for all figures.
The paragraph integrating the JAMA study is well-applied, showing effective use of a peer-reviewed source.
Linking the âHOLLYWOODâ billboard to the Camel lifestyle shows strong understanding of cultural connotation in visual rhetoric.
The interpretation of social smoking scene requires audience-response or survey data. Consider citing research on first-time smoking behaviors.
The low-angle shot analysis demonstrates accurate use of subject-specific visual terminology.
The term âmise-en-sceneâ is conflated with âvisual narrativeâ. Provide explicit definitions to avoid conceptual ambiguity.
The transition into copy analysis at figure 4 is abrupt; add a brief signpost to guide reader flow.
Insightful link between the âSmooth Characterâ slogan and product development goals; demonstrates strong analytical connection.
The range of primary and secondary sources in the Works Cited is commendable, showing effective research breadth.
Indentation in list items varies across citations. Standardize bullet formatting for consistency.
Claim that text-free images conceal advertising intent is intriguing but needs direct visual evidence; cite specific elements.
The comparative observation of dense versus sparse text in figures 1 and 5 shows effective depth in comparative visual analysis.
Characterize the campaignâs use of color frameworks with reference to design theory or secondary literature on attention economics.
The Vuse Trust campaign introduction sets context but omits dates for primary ads; specify time frame of artifacts.
Analysis of âKnow Your Vapeâ effectively links copy to audience reassurance, demonstrating nuanced critical thinking.
Consider integrating branding theory (e.g., Kellerâs brand equity) to underpin claims about building consumer trust.
The rule-of-thirds discussion illustrates technical layout knowledge; demonstrates accurate use of visual design concepts.
Define the ârule of thirdsâ briefly for readers unfamiliar with photographic principles to enhance accessibility.
Comparative Analysis synthesizes similarities and differences effectively, showing strong critical synthesis across campaigns.
Long paragraphs impede readability. Break into shorter sections or add sub-headings to improve structure.
The inclusivity discussion (gender, race) demonstrates contextual awareness of evolving social norms in advertising.
Substantiate claims about increased regulation by referencing specific legislation or guidelines restricting natural settings.
The conclusion acknowledges complexity by stating a definitive answer cannot be reached, reflecting appropriate caution in abstract inquiry.
Works Cited entries omit consistent hanging indents; apply uniform MLA formatting to each entry.
Ensure URLs in citations do not break mid-line by adjusting line spacing or using shorter URLs.
